channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: A deadly flight out of Wichita has one of America's most historic aviation cities reeling
item.description: WICHITA, Kan. (AP) — When American Eagle Flight 5342 took off from Wichita, the Midwest city with a proud aviation history was glowing from a big moment. It had just hosted the next generation of Olympic hopefuls at the U.S.
item.dc:creator: JOHN HANNA and HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Colleges around the US cautiously navigate Trump's DEI crackdown
item.description: Around the United States, colleges and universities are cautiously navigating the Trump administration's crackdown on diversity, equity and inclusion initiatives.
item.dc:creator: Maryclaire Dale, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: A medical plane carrying a child patient and 5 others crashes in Philadelphia, setting homes ablaze
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — A medical transport jet with a child patient, her mother and four others aboard crashed into a Philadelphia neighborhood shortly after takeoff Friday evening, exploding in a fireball that engulfed several homes.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and MATT ROURKE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Trump administration fires prosecutors involved in Jan. 6 cases and moves toward ousting FBI agents
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration on Friday fired a group of prosecutors involved in the Jan.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Air traffic controllers union responds to Trump's DEI attacks
item.description: The air traffic controllers union responded Friday to claims by President Trump this week that diversity programs contributed to the fatal crash in Washington Wednesday night.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Giorno, WGN 9

item.title: A list of government web pages that have gone dark to comply with Trump orders
item.description: A number of U.S. government web pages changed or went dark Friday as agencies scrambled to comply with President Donald Trump's executive orders declaring his administration would recognize only two genders and ordering an end to diversity, equity and inclusion initiatives.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN J. COOPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Medical plane carrying pediatric patient crashes in Philadelphia, sets homes ablaze
item.description: A medical transport jet headed for Missouri and carrying two people and four crew members crashed in Northeast Philadelphia Friday night.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: CBS agrees to hand over '60 Minutes' Harris interview transcripts to FCC
item.description: CBS says it will turn over an unedited transcript of its October interview with Kamala Harris to the Federal Communications Commission, part of President Donald Trump's ongoing fight with the network over how it handled a story about his opponent.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Latest snowpack survey reveals California could be in for extremely dry year
item.description: California’s snowpack has fallen well below average following an extremely dry January, according to the Department of Water Resources’ second snow survey of the season. The survey was conducted Friday at Phillips Station in the Sierra Nevada near Sacramento.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: DC plane crash: Potomac River divers' search for bodies complicated by conditions out of their control
item.description: After an Army helicopter and American Airlines plane crashed into the Potomac River around Washington, D.C., a Virginia diver shared challenges of such a recovery effort.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Soldiers in deadly helicopter crash with DC plane identified
item.description: The U.S. Army has identified two of the three soldiers who were killed Wednesday night when their helicopter crashed into an airplane in D.C.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump refuses to say if he’s spoken to Putin; White House ‘talking’ about ending Ukraine war
item.description: President Trump on Friday wouldn’t say if he has spoken with Russian President Vladimir Putin since he began his second term but said there are ongoing discussions about ending the war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WGN 9

item.title: Trump vows sweeping tariffs on oil, pharmaceuticals and semiconductors
item.description: President Trump on Friday vowed to impose sweeping tariffs on semiconductor chips, pharmaceuticals, steel and aluminum and oil and gas.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Air traffic controller left early from work before crash: report
item.description: An air traffic controller at Reagan National Airport reportedly left early on Wednesday before the deadly collision between an American Airlines flight and a military helicopter.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Arrest warrant issued for New York doctor indicted in Louisiana for prescribing abortion pill
item.description: An arrest warrant has been issued for a New York doctor indicted by a Louisiana grand jury for allegedly prescribing an abortion pill online to a pregnant minor.
item.dc:creator: Sara Cline And Geoff Mulvihill, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Flight recorders recovered in DCA crash
item.description: The National Transportation Safety Board (NTSB) said Thursday that they recovered flight data recorders, known as black boxes, from the aircraft involved in the deadly mid-air collision near Reagan Washington National Airport earlier this week.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, WGN 9

item.title: Trump warns Canada, Mexico tariffs are coming on Saturday
item.description: President Donald Trump on Thursday said he plans to follow through on Saturday on his threat to impose 25 percent tariffs on goods from Mexico and Canada.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: Two planes aborted landings at DCA due to helicopters in flight path in week before crash: report
item.description: Two planes aborted landings at Reagan National Airport in the past week due to military helicopters in their flight path, according to the Washington Post.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DC plane crash: Recovery continues, NTSB says investigation ongoing
item.description: Air crash investigations can take months, and federal investigators told reporters they would not speculate on the cause.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: South Carolina puts inmate Marion Bowman Jr. to death in state’s third execution since September
item.description: South Carolina has has put a third inmate to death in four months as it goes through a backlog of prisoners who exhausted their appeals while the state couldn’t find lethal injection drugs.
item.dc:creator: Jeffrey Collins, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Mushers and dogs braved a horrific Alaska winter to deliver lifesaving serum 100 years ago
item.description: ANCHORAGE, Alaska (AP) — The Alaska Gold Rush town of Nome faced a bleak winter. It was hundreds of miles from anywhere, cut off by the frozen sea and unrelenting blizzards, and under siege from a contagious disease known as the “strangling angel” for the way it suffocated children.
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Top music acts announced for FireAid, a concert to support L.A. wildfire victims
item.description: The biggest names in pop, rock and country are coming together to raise funds for victims of the Palisades and Eaton wildfires and assist with the recovery.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, NewsNation

item.title: Engineering student among DC plane crash victims
item.description: DAYTON, Ohio (WDTN) -- Cedarville University is grieving one of its own after the devastating plane crash in Washington D.C. Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Emily Lewis, WFLA, NBC

item.title: MMA fighter praises Hitler, calls him a 'good guy' in podcast
item.description: UFC fighter Bryce Mitchell praised Adolf Hitler and denied the Holocaust happened in the first episode of his new podcast.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, KTLA 5

item.title: FCC chair launches investigation into NPR, PBS
item.description: The chairman of the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) is launching an investigation into NPR and PBS over their alleged "airing of commercials.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, Fox 5

item.title: Midwest woman pardoned for Jan. 6 offenses sentenced in deadly drunken-driving crash
item.description: Emily Hernandez, a Missouri woman pardoned after her ties to the Jan. 6, 2021, insurrection at the U.S. Capitol, was sentenced Wednesday in connection with a drunken-driving crash.
item.dc:creator: Joey Schneider, WGN 9

item.title: Tennessee GOP passes school voucher expansion bill with backing from Trump
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — Tennessee lawmakers on Thursday approved legislation drastically expanding the number of families who can use taxpayer money on private schools regardless of income, a long-sought victory for Republican Gov.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN MATTISE and KIMBERLEE KRUESI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: FCC chair launches investigation into NPR, PBS
item.description: "I am concerned that NPR and PBS broadcasts could be violating federal law by airing commercials," FCC Chair Brendan Carr wrote.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, NewsNation

item.title: No Super Bowl rally if Kansas City Chiefs beat Philadelphia Eagles: Sources
item.description: Multiple sources have confirmed that should the Kansas City Chiefs win Super Bowl LIX, there will be no rally for the public.
item.dc:creator: Alyssa Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: NFL kicker Justin Tucker accused of sexual misconduct by 6 masseuses: report
item.description: Justin Tucker, kicker for the Baltimore Ravens, has been accused by six massage therapists at four separate businesses of sexually inappropriate behavior, the Baltimore Banner reported Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, NewsNation

item.title: Missing Colorado juvenile found in Houston; Suspect arrested by Bellaire police
item.description: BELLAIRE, Texas (KIAH) – A missing juvenile from Colorado has been safely recovered in Houston, thanks to the swift action by the Bellaire Police Department.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Estrada, Fox 5

item.title: Video captures fiery crash between American Airlines flight and Army Blackhawk
item.description: Editor's note: The video may appear graphic to some viewers. (NEXSTAR) - Images from the EarthCam video service captured images of what appears to be the fiery crash of an American Airlines jet over the Potomac River Wednesday evening.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, WFLA, NBC

item.title: What we know about the DC plane crash victims
item.description: Among those killed were figure skaters, union members, and a couple en route to visit their child at college.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, WFLA, NBC

item.title: No survivors after collision between American Airlines jet, Army helicopter near DC
item.description: Everyone aboard an American Airlines jet carrying 60 passengers and four crew members that collided with an Army helicopter was feared dead in what was likely to be the worst U.S. aviation disaster in almost a quarter century, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Anna Chen, ABC 4

item.title: Are we all aliens? NASA's returned asteroid samples hold the ingredients of life from a watery world
item.description: Asteroid samples fetched by NASA hold not only the pristine building blocks for life but also the salty remains of an ancient water world..
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5
